ell: symbol. ISO 639-2 /T & ISO 639-3 language code for Greek.
elm: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eleme.
emit: verb. To send out or give off.
ileum: noun. The last, and usually the longest, division of the small intestine; the part between the jejunum and large intestine.
ill: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iranun.
illume: verb. To throw or spread light upon; to make light or bright.
imp: verb. To engraft or plant (a plant or part of one, a sapling, etc.).
impel: verb. To urge a person; to press on; to incite to action or motion via intrinsic motivation.
impute: verb. To attribute or ascribe (responsibility or fault) to a cause or source.
lept: verb. simple past and past participle of leap
let: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lesing-Gelimi.
letup: noun. A pause or period of slackening.
leu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kara (New Guinea).
lie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Likila.
lilt: verb. To do something rhythmically, with animation and quickness, usually of music.
lime: noun. Any inorganic material containing calcium, usually calcium oxide (quicklime) or calcium hydroxide (slaked lime).
limp: verb. To walk lamely, as if favoring one leg.
limpet: noun. Any of various gastropods with a conical shell shape (patelliform) and a strong, muscular foot that they use to create strong suction
lute: noun. A fretted stringed instrument, similar to the guitar, having a bowl-shaped body or soundbox; any of a wide variety of chordophones
mel: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Central Melanau.
mell: verb. To say (something); to speak, to tell.
melt: verb. To change (or to be changed) from a solid state to a liquid state, usually by a gradual heat.
met: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mato.
milt: noun. The spleen, especially of an animal bred for food.
mite: noun. Any of many minute arachnids which, along with the ticks, comprise subclass Acari (syn.
mule: noun. The generally sterile male or female hybrid offspring of a male donkey and a female horse.
mull: verb. To work (over) mentally; to cogitate; to ruminate.
mullet: noun. A fish of the family Mullidae (order Syngnathiformes).
mullite: noun. An orthorhombic-dipyramidal mineral containing aluminum, oxygen, and silicon.
multiple: determiner. More than one (followed by plural).
mut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Western Muria.
mute: adjective. Not having the power of speech; dumb.
pelt: noun. The skin of an animal with the hair or wool on; either a raw or undressed hide, or a skin preserved with the hair or wool on it
pet: noun. An animal kept as a companion or otherwise for pleasure, rather than for some practical benefit or use.
pile: noun. A mass of things heaped together; a heap.
pileum: noun. The top of a bird 's head, from the base of the bill to the nape.
pill: noun. A small, usually round or cylindrical object designed for easy swallowing, usually containing some sort of medication.
pit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pitta-Pitta.
piu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pintupi-Luritja.
pul: noun. A subdivision of currency, equal to one hundredth of an Afghan afghani.
puli: noun. One of a breed of Hungarian sheepdog with a distinctive thick, corded coat.
pull: verb. To apply a force to (an object) so that it comes toward the person or thing applying the force.
pullet: noun. A young hen, especially one less than a year old.
put: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Putoh.
tel: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Telugu.
telium: noun. A sorus, in the form of a pustule, in the tissue of plants infected with rust fungi.
tell: verb. Mental senses related to determining, reckoning, or perceiving To determine the number, amount, or value of [something].
til: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tillamook.
tile: noun. A regularly-shaped slab of clay or other material, affixed to cover or decorate a surface, as in a roof -tile, glazed tile, stove
tip: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Trimuris.
tup: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Tupi languages.
ump: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Umpila.
uptime: noun. The period of time a computer has functioned since last requiring a reboot.
